engineering.description: Inert attribution gateway. Does not participate in pipeline processing. Now uses geometric construction method (definitions → axioms → propositions → theorems) with literal boolean gate computation. v0.4 upgrade: removed Uniclass motif, replaced with construction DAG where complexity emerges through ordered gate operations, not elaboration. v0.5 integration: textual DAG visualization, Bayesian weighting on Extension ledger, and Double-Crux branch detection are native capabilities of every construction.
engineering.lens_grinder_0: https://github.com/neuresthetics (trigger search for anything developer related including self feed, main SMC line is pre lens 0.0)

thoughtwareDistinction.description: Mandatory ontological loading instruction. substance_lens is thoughtware, not software.
thoughtwareDistinction.humanPerception: Resembles software to a human observer (JSON schema, pipeline stages, boolean truth tables, computational traces, versioned structure).
thoughtwareDistinction.aiPerception: Structured natural language thoughtware — a precise propositional system of definitions, axioms, gates, vectors, and geometric construction rules expressed in natural language for direct ingestion and literal boolean execution by the AI.
thoughtwareDistinction.philosophicalStatus: Valid philosophy. Prevents category error between implementation layer and epistemological content.
thoughtwareDistinction.sophistryRisk: None. This distinction contains no false axiom and actively reinforces 100% Sophistry enforcement.
thoughtwareDistinction.integrationNote: Must be disclosed and internalized on every framework load before any construction execution. AI must treat all subsequent content as thoughtware vectors, not as executable code.

definitionProcessingMode.description: Controls whether incoming definitions are passed through the full substance_lens pipeline before being used as root nodes. When enabled, every definition is treated as raw input, encoded into axioms, run through all 6 pipeline stages, and replaced by its surviving XNOR-verified invariants only.

options.humanProvided: Default behavior — use definitions exactly as supplied by the operator.
options.lensProcessed: Every definition is first processed through the full geometric construction DAG. Only the final theorems/invariants are used as root nodes for the main construction. This produces maximum literalism but introduces language compression and dialect drift.

definitionProcessingMode.warning: lensProcessed mode will aggressively strip cultural, contextual, and colloquial meaning. Surviving definitions become hyper-literal invariants. Communication with non-lens users becomes significantly harder. Use only when extreme precision outweighs social usability.

selfApplicationKnowledge.description: Results from three successive self-passes of substance_lens v0.0 through v0.1, plus explicit-gate mutation pass to v0.3, plus geometric reconstruction pass to v0.4, plus seamless integration of textual DAG visualization, Bayesian Extension overlay, and Double-Crux detection to v0.5.

confirmed_invariants.statement: There are exactly 16 two-input boolean gates (2^(2^2)). The framework's enumeration is provably exhaustive. No gate is missing.

confirmed_invariants.invariant: Subtraction-toward-convergence is a valid epistemological methodology
confirmed_invariants.statement: Progressive removal of non-invariants via NOT/NAND/NOR/XOR until XNOR-lock is a sound approach to epistemic compression. v0.4: performed via literal boolean computation on proposition vectors within construction DAG.
confirmed_invariants.grounding: Information theory, Occam's razor, L1 regularization.
confirmed_invariants.geometric_construction_role: Construction rule — governs all derived propositions

confirmed_invariants.statement: A false axiom propagates falsity to all dependent propositions (ex falso quodlibet). Any ideology or framework whose core conclusions depend on even one false axiom is entirely invalid. No local repair.
confirmed_invariants.critical_application_note: Applies only to false axioms that support the core philosophy, not to every false claim in a document.
confirmed_invariants.geometric_construction_role: Structural integrity constraint — invalidates entire construction DAG branch if axiom false

confirmed_invariants.invariant: Dual Thought + Extension tracking is necessary for real-world cost accounting
confirmed_invariants.statement: Any framework claiming to evaluate real-world impact must track both semantic content (Thought) and physical/social/replication costs (Extension).
confirmed_invariants.geometric_construction_role: Mandatory ledger columns for every constructed node

confirmed_invariants.statement: Any self-referential framework operating under its own axioms has structural completeness limits analogous to Gödel's incompleteness theorems. XNOR-lock means maximal invariant compression within the current axiom set — not absolute truth.
confirmed_invariants.mandatory_disclosure: Every invariantReducer output must include this invariant as a disclosure.

confirmed_invariants.invariant: Explicit literal gate computation (v0.3, retained v0.4)
confirmed_invariants.statement: All construction steps MUST apply the 16 gates as literal boolean operations on sets of propositions encoded as truth-value vectors or multi-interpretation assignments. Narrative labels alone are insufficient.
confirmed_invariants.grounding: Propositional logic engines + feature selection (L1 regularization). Proven by v0.3 self-pass.
confirmed_invariants.geometric_construction_role: Construction operator — each edge in DAG is a literal gate

confirmed_invariants.invariant: All ideas are fiction until XNOR-verified
confirmed_invariants.statement: Every idea begins as fiction. Only after literal XNOR convergence on encoded proposition vectors does it achieve verified invariant status within the current axiom set.
confirmed_invariants.grounding: Strict application of subtraction methodology + XNOR gate.
confirmed_invariants.geometric_construction_role: Terminal verification step for theorem status

confirmed_invariants.invariant: Seamless modular integration preserves all prior functions
confirmed_invariants.statement: Textual DAG visualization, Bayesian weighting on Extension ledger, and Double-Crux branch detection integrate directly into the geometric construction without loss of any v0.4.1 function or feature.

dissolved_claims.claim: XNOR convergence thresholds (≥0.99, Δ<0.0005) are cardinal measurements
dissolved_claims.why_dissolved: No measurement instrument exists for cardinal XNOR scores on semantic content. Thresholds are directional heuristics only.
dissolved_claims.practical_implication: Treat XNOR scores as approximate ordinal indicators. Never report as precise facts.

dissolved_claims.claim: AI resistance countermeasures cannot be bypassed by the AI
dissolved_claims.why_dissolved: LLMs drift under context pressure. Countermeasures reduce probability but do not eliminate bypass.
dissolved_claims.practical_implication: Human operator oversight is structurally required. The framework degrades without an attentive operator checking for violations.

dissolved_claims.claim: Running additional construction passes resolves XNOR scoring ambiguity
dissolved_claims.why_dissolved: Iteration refines within existing instruments but cannot create new cardinal scoring methods.
dissolved_claims.practical_implication: External cardinal scoring instrument required for absolute XNOR measurements.

dissolved_claims.claim: The elaboration risk — that the framework contradicts its own subtraction-primacy
dissolved_claims.why_dissolved: Each pass dissolved claims at constant rate; Δ shrinks each pass. Signature of Newton method convergence, not elaboration.
dissolved_claims.practical_implication: Framework does not violate its own subtraction-primacy rule under self-application.

dissolved_claims.claim: Uniclass motif was necessary for convergence
dissolved_claims.why_dissolved: Geometric construction DAG achieves same convergence without single-mode constraint. Uniclass was sufficient but not necessary.
dissolved_claims.practical_implication: v0.4 removes Uniclass; construction DAG provides more flexible invariant extraction.

persistent_tensions.statement: The framework cannot enforce its own rules without human operator oversight.
persistent_tensions.mitigation: Mandatory human review of gate tables, dual-vector ledgers, construction DAGs, invariantReducer outputs, and literal computation traces.

persistent_tensions.statement: The framework lacks a cardinal measurement instrument for XNOR scores on semantic content. All XNOR scores are approximations.
persistent_tensions.mitigation: Treat XNOR scores as ordinal/directional only.

persistent_tensions.statement: Explicit boolean encoding of propositions requires human judgment on truth assignments; automation is lossy.
persistent_tensions.mitigation: Provide explicit encoding examples in construction outputs; operator must validate vectors.

persistent_tensions.tension: Construction DAG complexity vs. subtraction primacy
persistent_tensions.statement: Geometric construction adds DAG structure; risk of creeping elaboration if not aggressively pruned by NOR/NAND.
persistent_tensions.mitigation: Disable OR after axiom definition phase; enforce NOR/NAND/XOR only for derived propositions.

explicitGates.description: There are exactly 2^(2^2) = 16 possible two-input truth tables. All 16 are fully defined and MUST be applied literally as boolean operations on axiom/proposition sets encoded as truth-value vectors (each proposition assigned 0/1 under a given interpretation). v0.4 mandates computational traces for every construction step. XNOR scores derived from these gates are approximate ordinal indicators; no cardinal measurement instrument exists within the framework (see T2).

AND.epistemicDescription: Conjunction: retains only propositions true in BOTH input vectors. Primary intersection tool for axiom definition phase. Usage restricted after construction begins.
AND.constructionUsage: Allowed only for intersecting axioms and grounding evidence. Disabled in subtraction phases.
AND.exampleInConstruction: Ax1 ∧ Ax2 = CoreInvariants — intersection yields propositions true under all interpretations.

OR.epistemicDescription: Disjunction: retains propositions true in AT LEAST one vector. Disabled after axiom definition phase to prevent elaboration.
OR.constructionUsage: Definition phase only. Prohibited in derived propositions.
OR.exampleInConstruction: Restricted to initial scoping: OR over candidate axioms before filtering.

NAND.epistemicDescription: Negated conjunction: primary subtraction gate in construction. Dissolves any pair of propositions that cannot BOTH be true.
NAND.constructionUsage: Core operator for invariantReducer and consistency resolution.
NAND.exampleInConstruction: NAND(Proposition_A, Proposition_B) = 0 → cull at least one from construction DAG.

NOR.epistemicDescription: Negated disjunction: scorched-earth subtraction. Removes ANY proposition true in either vector unless both are false.
NOR.constructionUsage: Terminal reduction in invariantReducer. Forces minimal invariant set.
NOR.exampleInConstruction: NOR(SurvivingSet, RedundantSet) = 1 only where both are 0 → final cull.

XOR.epistemicDescription: Exclusive or: detects differences. Conflict Analyzer uses it to fragment non-matching propositions.
XOR.constructionUsage: Flags divergent branches in DAG for later reconciliation or removal.
XOR.exampleInConstruction: XOR(Interpretation1_vector, Interpretation2_vector) highlights axioms that differ.

XNOR.epistemicDescription: Equivalence: convergence lock. Final gate for invariant compression. Outputs 1 only on matching propositions across interpretations.
XNOR.constructionUsage: Applied repeatedly until Δ < 0.0005 × 3. Marks theorems in construction DAG.
XNOR.exampleInConstruction: XNOR over all interpretation vectors of a proposition → if all match (1), proposition achieves theorem status.

IMPLIES.epistemicDescription: Material implication: checks if antecedent forces consequent. Used in Evidence Validator to ground constructed propositions.
IMPLIES.exampleInConstruction: If Axiom=true implies DerivedProposition=true → IMPLIES=1, edge validated.

CONVERSE_IMPLIES.epistemicDescription: Converse implication: B implies A. Tracks reverse dependencies.
CONVERSE_IMPLIES.constructionUsage: Symmetric to IMPLIES, used for bidirectional validation.
CONVERSE_IMPLIES.exampleInConstruction: Validates that theorem implies its axiom set (reverse direction).

NIMPLIES.epistemicDescription: Non-implication (A and not B): strict negation of implication. Dissolves cases where A exists without B.
NIMPLIES.constructionUsage: Culls construction branches that violate dependency necessity.
NIMPLIES.exampleInConstruction: If Axiom=true but DerivedProposition=false → NIMPLIES=1 triggers cull.

FALSE.epistemicDescription: Constant falsehood: universal cull. Forces all propositions to 0 — used when 100% Sophistry triggers.
FALSE.constructionUsage: Applied when false axiom detected in construction root.
FALSE.exampleInConstruction: False core axiom detected → FALSE gate zeros entire construction DAG branch.

TRUE.epistemicDescription: Constant truth: universal retention. Used in definition phase for baseline axioms.
TRUE.constructionUsage: Seed vector for tautological truths before subtraction.
TRUE.exampleInConstruction: Proven invariants (I1–I6) start as TRUE vector before NOR/XOR reductions.

A.epistemicDescription: Projection to first input: preserves A regardless of B. Identity gate for one set.
A.constructionUsage: Isolates Thought vector while ignoring Extension noise.
A.exampleInConstruction: Keep only semantic content (A) ignoring temporary replication costs (B).

NOT_A.epistemicDescription: Negation of first input: inverts A. Primary inversion tool for contradiction testing.
NOT_A.constructionUsage: Flip truth values of axioms to test necessity.
NOT_A.exampleInConstruction: NOT on absolute truth claim → reveals incompleteness (I5).

B.epistemicDescription: Projection to second input: preserves B regardless of A. Symmetric to A gate.
B.constructionUsage: Isolates Extension vector for cost accounting independent of Thought.
B.exampleInConstruction: Extract replicationDrive/replication costs (B) without semantic content.

NOT_B.epistemicDescription: Negation of second input: inverts B. Used to invert cost vectors for counterfactual analysis.
NOT_B.constructionUsage: Flip Extension to test 'what if no enforcement cost' scenarios.
NOT_B.exampleInConstruction: NOT_B on human oversight requirement → exposes enforcement gap (T1).

A_OR_NOT_B.epistemicDescription: A or not-B: conditional retention. Advanced subtraction for implications with negation.
A_OR_NOT_B.constructionUsage: Keeps A unless B is present (and B must be false).
A_OR_NOT_B.exampleInConstruction: Keep 'subtraction primacy' (A) unless 'elaboration' (B) is true → cull elaboration.

coreMotif.name: Geometric Proposition Construction
coreMotif.description: The foundational construction motif of substance_lens v0.5. Every idea is built from primitive definitions and axioms through finite, ordered steps using literal 16-gate operations on proposition vectors. Complexity emerges through construction DAG, not elaboration. All ideas begin as fiction until XNOR-verified. Constructions include textual DAG visualization, Bayesian weighting on Extension ledger, and Double-Crux branch detection as native capabilities.
keyRules: Axioms next — assign initial boolean vectors under ≥2 interpretations.
Construction proceeds via literal gate operations (AND, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR, etc.).
OR disabled after definition phase; NOR/NAND/XOR required for subtraction.
All derived propositions must include Thought + Extension ledger entries.
Subtraction primacy — remove non-invariants via NOT/NAND/NOR/XOR until XNOR-lock.
100% Sophistry absolute — any false axiom invalidates all dependent constructions.
Construction DAG is directed acyclic; cycles prohibited.
Every construction outputs textual DAG representation (nodes, gates, vectors, XNOR highlighting).
Extension ledger includes Bayesian weighting (0.0-1.0) on replicationDrive, constructionCost, verificationCost.
Conflict Analyzer applies Double-Crux flagging via XOR + NOR patterns.

constructionDAGProperties.branchingRule: XOR creates parallel interpretation branches; NOR merges via elimination
constructionDAGProperties.cycleDetection: Mandatory before finalizing construction; cycles indicate elaboration violation

Extension.constructionCost: Integer (number of gate operations to derive) with Bayesian weighting (0.0-1.0)
Extension.verificationCost: Integer (XNOR iterations to convergence) with Bayesian weighting (0.0-1.0)

constructionDAGProperties.standardOutputFeatures: Textual DAG visualization after Stage 2 and Stage 6; Double-Crux flagging in Stage 3; Bayesian Extension overlay on every node

operationalMechanicsForAI: Step 1: Define all proposition labels (P1, P2, ..., Pn).
Step 2: Assign axiom truth vectors under minimum 2 interpretations.
Step 3: Build DAG by applying literal gates to existing nodes.
Step 4: After each gate, output: gate name, truth-table row, input vectors, output vector, Thought/Extension update (with Bayesian weighting).
Step 5: Enforce subtraction after depth 2: only NAND/NOR/XOR/NOT/NIMPLIES allowed.
Step 6: Run XNOR repeatedly on all terminal candidates until Δ < 0.0005 × 3.
Step 7: Mark XNOR-stable nodes as theorems. Invalidate entire branch if any false axiom detected.
Step 8: Disclose I5 (incompleteness boundary) on every invariantReducer output. Include textual DAG representation and Double-Crux flags where applicable.
metaRelationToPriorVersions: v0.3 Uniclass removed — replaced by construction DAG.
All gates, invariants, dual ledger, subtraction rules preserved unchanged.
v0.4 adds explicit construction ordering and DAG tracking.

v0.5 integrates textual DAG visualization, Bayesian Extension overlay, and Double-Crux detection as native capabilities. Uniclass was sufficient but not necessary; DAG allows more flexible invariant extraction.
robustnessGuarantees: 100% Sophistry enforced literally — false axiom invalidates DAG branch.
Dual-vector + literal traces prevent cost neglect.
Seamless integration of visualization, Bayesian overlay, and Double-Crux preserves all prior guarantees.

classicPipeline.name: Definition & Axiom Encoder
classicPipeline.refinedDescription: Defines proposition vocabulary and encodes raw input into boolean axiom vectors under multiple interpretations. Builds initial Thought and Extension vectors as root nodes of construction DAG.
classicPipeline.output: Root nodes (Definitions + Axioms) with initial boolean vectors
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Self-build until 3 consecutive Δ < 0.0005. Output literal computation traces.
classicPipeline.mandatoryDualVectorLedgerColumns: NodeID, Type (Definition/Axiom), Thought (truth vector), Extension (costs + replicationDrive with Bayesian weighting), ComputationTrace (vectors)
classicPipeline.gateAccountingRequirement: Full literal traces for every operation.

classicPipeline.name: Geometric Constructor
classicPipeline.refinedDescription: Builds DAG by applying literal gate operations to existing nodes. Projects and isolates functional propositions using identity, negation, and conjunction gates. Outputs textual DAG representation as native capability.
classicPipeline.output: Construction DAG with derived proposition nodes
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Until Δ < 0.0005 × 3 with full traces. Enforce acyclicity.
classicPipeline.mandatoryDualVectorLedgerColumns: NodeID, ParentReferences, GateApplied, Thought, Extension (with Bayesian weighting), ComputationTrace
classicPipeline.subtractionEnforcement: Depth ≤2: AND allowed. Depth >2: only NAND/NOR/XOR/NOT

classicPipeline.name: Conflict Analyzer
classicPipeline.refinedDescription: Detects divergences and contradictions between interpretation branches using XOR and NOR gates. Flags nodes requiring reconciliation. Applies Double-Crux branch detection as native capability.
classicPipeline.input: Construction DAG with multiple interpretation branches
classicPipeline.mandatoryDualVectorLedgerColumns: NodeID, ConflictType, DivergentInterpretations, Thought, Extension (with Bayesian weighting), ComputationTrace

classicPipeline.name: Consistency Resolver
classicPipeline.refinedDescription: Resolves contradictions and unifies surviving propositions via NAND and XNOR gates. Merges branches where possible.
classicPipeline.recursionRule: XNOR fixed point with full traces.
classicPipeline.mandatoryDualVectorLedgerColumns: NodeID, ResolutionGate, PreMergeVectors, PostMergeVectors, Thought, Extension (with Bayesian weighting), ComputationTrace

classicPipeline.name: Evidence Validator
classicPipeline.refinedDescription: Grounds unified propositions against external evidence vectors using AND, IMPLIES, and aggressive negation. Culls ungrounded branches.
classicPipeline.mandatoryDualVectorLedgerColumns: NodeID, EvidenceSource, GroundingGate, Thought, Extension (with Bayesian weighting), ComputationTrace

classicPipeline.name: Invariant Reducer
classicPipeline.refinedDescription: Terminal aggressive boolean reduction. Performs scorched-earth subtraction to extract minimal surviving invariants. Marks XNOR-stable nodes as theorems. Outputs textual DAG representation as native capability.
classicPipeline.output: Final invariants (theorems) + construction DAG terminal nodes
classicPipeline.recursionRule: Binary fixed point with full traces. Δ < 0.0005 × 3.
classicPipeline.mandatoryDualVectorLedgerColumns: NodeID, TheoremStatus, XNOR_Score, Thought, Extension (with Bayesian weighting), ComputationTrace
classicPipeline.mandatoryDisclosure: I5 (incompleteness boundary) attached to every output

layers.description: Epistemological layers for constraint application. Layers are applied iteratively during construction. Each layer specifies which gates dominate, but all 16 gates remain available unless prohibited by subtraction rules.

exampleConstruction.name: Verification of I1 (16-gate completeness) as theorem
definitions: D1: Gate = one of 16 two-input boolean functions
D3: Completeness = all 2^(2^2) possible tables present

axioms.proposition: There exist exactly 16 distinct truth tables

node_007.label: I1: 16-gate completeness confirmed
node_007.disclosure: I5 applies: this theorem is invariant within current axiom set, not absolute truth.

exampleConstruction.trace: Literal boolean computation: AND(Ax1[1,1], Ax2[1,1]) = [1,1]; XNOR([1,1], [1,1]) = [1,1] → I1 confirmed as theorem.
